steps.title: Make the first two model points
steps.content: The starting population is 24, so use $(0,24)$. A 16.2% yearly increase means one year later the population is $24(1.162)=27.888$, so use $(1,27.888)$. Fit an exponential regression.

steps.title: Compare to the answer choices
steps.content: Choice C has the correct starting population as the coefficient and the annual growth factor as the base. Choice A uses 24 as the base, choice B uses a doubling-style model, and choice D raises the product to the $t$ power, which would make the starting value 1. Choice C is correct.
